reference: Hi, and welcome to the health care management. This sounds like gallbladder disorders. It is most commonly caused by stones and aligns with the symptoms you are describing, especially if the pain is aggravated by meals and fatty foods. This is a crampy, colicky kind of pain that is not constant, and it is usually associated with nausea and vomiting. To evaluate the gallbladder, you should undergo an ultrasound and liver enzyme level tests. After this, a cholecystectomy can be performed. Until that time, you should avoid fatty foods and alcohol. However, you should consider some other disorders such as ureteral stones and pancreatitis. Ureteral stones can relate to your urination difficulties. I wish you good health.
text: Hi and welcome to the healthcare management. This sounds like gallbladder disorders. It is most commonly caused by stones and aligns with the symptoms you are describing, especially if the pain is aggravated by meals and fatties. This is a crampy, colicky kind of pain that is not constant, and it is usually associated with nausea and vomiting. To evaluate the gallbladder, you should undergo an ultrasound and liver enzyme level tests. After this, a cholecystectomy can be performed. Until that time, you should avoid fatty foods and alcohol. However, you should consider some other disorders such as ureteral stones and pancreatitis. Ureteral stones can relate to your urination difficulties. I wish you good health.
